== Notes for [[Item:Q111387|Q111387]] ==

He was not the son of [http://www.geni.com/people/Jason-Hasmonean/6000000006101322247 Jason Maccabee] or grandson of [http://www.geni.com/people/Elazar-Maccabeus-%D7%90%D7%9C%D7%A2%D7%96%D7%A8-%D7%94%D7%9E%D7%9B%D7%91%D7%99-Maccabeus/6000000012127639504 Elazar Maccabeus]. Sir Anthony Richard Wagner, ''Pedigree and Progress: Essays in the Genealogical Interpretation of History'' (1975) lists Antipater I's father as Herodes b c148 BCE.

"Antipas, a convert to Judaism, who was governor of Idumæa under the reigns of Alexander Jannæus and his queen dowager Alexandra, and rendered himself serviceable to the Jewish rulers, through his connections with his former congeners, of Arabia Petræa." (Source: [http://www.jewishencyclopedia.com/articles/1598-antipater Antipater] in ''Jewish Encyclopedia'')

>>><nowiki>-------------</nowiki>
Wagner, Sir Anthony Richard; ‘Pedigree and Progress: Essays in the Genealogical Interpretation of History’ estimates birth date as c148BC and does not call him Antipas Hasmonai, but '''Antipater'''

>>>________

Interesting conversation on possibility that he was a Hasmonean here: http://groups.yahoo.com/group/ancient_genealogy/message/445

>>><nowiki>-----------------</nowiki>

See [http://www.geni.com/discussions/151163?msg=1053520 Discussion] of his birthdate

SOURCE NOTES:
http://www.american-pictures.com/genealogy/persons/per01345.htm#0

RESEARCH NOTES:
Governor of Idumaea

GOVERNOR OF IDUMAEA

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
